title: Generate Text with LLM (MLX)
cap_description: Generate text using MLX-based LLM models on Apple Silicon
documentation:
## Purpose

Accepts a raw text prompt and generates a text continuation using an MLX-optimized LLM on Apple Silicon. Simple prompt-in / text-out interface for fast local generation on Mac M-series hardware.

## Inputs

- A text prompt (plain text).
- A model spec (MLX HuggingFace repo or local path). Defaults to `Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct-4bit`.
- Sampling parameters: max tokens (512), temperature (0.7), top-p (0.9), repetition penalty (1.1), seed (required, default 0).

## Outputs

Generated text streamed as raw text chunks (not NDJSON).

## Notes

- Requires Apple Silicon (M1 or later).
- The seed parameter is required (unlike the other text generation caps where it is optional). Pass `0` for non-deterministic output.
- For structured output or streaming JSON events, use [LLM Text Generation (MLX)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:llm-generation-request;json;record";llm;ml-model;mlx;op=llm_inference;out="media:llm-text-stream;ndjson") instead.

args.arg_description: MLX model specification (HuggingFace model ID or local path)
HuggingFace bearer token. Required when targeting a gated repository.
Maximum number of tokens to generate

output.output_description: Generated text streamed as raw text chunks
